description: Example grid search config with industry-standard parameter ranges for ORB+ATR strategy optimization

overrides.description: Opening range duration (minutes) - Lower = more trades, Higher = better confirmation
Entry buffer beyond range (basis points) - 0 = no buffer, 10+ reduces false breakouts
Risk per trade (%) - Professionals use 1-2%, never exceed 5%
Stop loss ATR multiple - Lower = tighter stop, Higher = more room
Take profit R-multiple - 2R = conservative, 3R = balanced, 4R+ = aggressive

notes: 📚 FOR COMPLETE PARAMETER REFERENCE:
See ALL-PARAMETERS-REFERENCE.md for full list of overrideable parameters
with descriptions, value ranges, and examples!
HOW TO USE THIS CONFIG:
1. Modify 'symbols', 'intervals', 'dateRange', and 'initialEquity' for your test
2. Update 'baseParams' with your baseline strategy configuration
3. Add more override blocks from ALL-PARAMETERS-REFERENCE.md to test
4. Modify the 'values' arrays to test different parameter combinations
5. Run: npm run backtest:batch -- --config your-config.json
PERFORMANCE TUNING:
• concurrency: Number of parallel backtests (default: 10)
- Recommended: 1.5-2x your CPU core count for optimal speed
- Example: 8-core CPU → use 12-16 concurrency
- Higher than 2x cores = diminishing returns (CPU context switching overhead)
- You CAN go higher (e.g., 50+), but test to see if it's faster
- Each backtest is CPU-intensive, so more threads ≠ always faster
CALCULATION:
This example generates: 1 symbol × 2 intervals × (3×3×3×3×3) = 486 tests
With concurrency=10, expect ~1-2 minutes runtime (depends on your CPU)
WORKFLOW:
1. Start with 2-3 override parameters and small value ranges (100-500 tests)
2. Review results in Google Sheets to identify best performers
3. Refine ranges around winners and add more parameters to test
4. Repeat until you find optimal configuration
OUTPUT:
Results written to JSONL → Convert to CSV → Import to Google Sheets for analysis
